title: Used to
explanation:
Used to

Used to + verb — past habits or states that are no longer true.

  • I used to live in Rome. (I don't live there now.)
  • She didn't use to like coffee.
  • Did you use to play the piano?

Don't confuse with be used to + -ing (= be accustomed to).

questions.text: I [*used to walk|use to walk|am used to walk] to school every day.
She [*didn't use to|didn't used to|doesn't use to] eat meat.
There [*used to be|use to be|was used to be] a cinema in this street.
He [*used to have|uses to have|is used to have] long hair.
We [*didn't use to|not use to|don't use to] watch much TV.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: My grandfather ____ work in a factory. | *used to | uses to | is used to
I ____ play tennis, but I stopped. | *used to | use to | am used to
____ she use to speak French? | *Did | Does | Was
There used to ____ a park here. | *be | have | being
I [*am used to getting|used to get|use to get] up early now — it's normal for me.
They [*used to travel|use to travel|are used to travel] a lot before they had children.
He [*didn't use to|wasn't use to|doesn't use to] enjoy cooking.
[*Did|Used|Was] there use to be more shops here?
She [*used to work|is used to work|uses to work] in London.
